Author: Leif Moller
Date: May 30, 2026
Email: leif.moller12@gmail.com

Note: All extra resources (Excel spreadsheet, LapSim process) found in FE Drive at
    Aero -> First Year Sub Tasks -> Lap Sims



*** INSTALATIONS ***

Paste following command into terminal / command window:

    pip install numpy matplotlib pandas scipy



*** PURPOSE ***

Simulations run with Star CCM give output of drag coefficient (Cd) and lift coefficient (Cl) values
for aerodynamic configurations.

This code is used both to visualize trends of performance for differenct Cd and Cl values
in different race categories, and to predict point return values for specific Cd and Cl values.

These interpolated return values will guide aero kit design, as the aero team aims to maximize points in competition.

The lap times for Cd and Cl values were obtained iteravily using OptimumLap. More info on process in FE Drive
An Excel spreadsheet was then used to calculate predicted points in each category based on the 2026 FSAE rules for points

       Found at: https://www.fsaeonline.com/cdsweb/gen/DownloadDocument.aspx?DocumentID=278fd4d7-aa27-4e33-bc4a-090148e662a0




*** USING THE PROGRAM ***

Run CSV_Scraper first, then 3D Graph.

Prompts should guide decisions. 
Simply input Cd and Cl values when prompted (after selecting single graph).
Terminal will output exact interpolated point, while graph can be used for visualization.




*** ADJUSTMENTS ***

If rules change, equations in excel should be adjusted. The excel spreadsheet also uses max and minumum times from 2025
for point calculations. These should also be updated eventually. More info in spreadsheet found in FE Drive.

After making adjustments to excel spreadsheet, download and use the new lapTimeData. Code should still work as designed.
